struct/Struct/size.name: size
struct/Struct/size.description: The calculated size of the struct (and hence of the bytes object produced by the pack() method) corresponding to format.

info.excerpt: The calculated size of the struct (and hence of the bytes object produced by the pack() method) corresponding to format.

struct/Struct.name: Struct
struct/Struct.description: Return a new Struct object which writes and reads binary data according to the format string format. Creating a Struct object once and calling its methods is more efficient than calling module-level functions with the same format since the format string is only compiled once. Note The compiled versions of the most recent format strings passed to the module-level functions are cached, so programs that use only a few format strings needn’t worry about reusing a single Struct instance.
